detaileddescription.para: Max No of streams supported by logical camera.
x86 logical camera.
CV logical camera.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Logical camera identifiers available for access.
To capture an image.
To capture a video.
To capture a video and access the raw buffer of the frames.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
None, used for still capture.
Specified 15FPS.
Specified 30FPS.
Specified 60FPS.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

Call MLCameraPrepareCapture() to configure frame rate. Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_None when configuring only Image capture. Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_60fps only when resolution of captures <= 3MP(2048x1536). Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_15FPS only when MLCameraConnectFlag is MLCameraConnectFlag_CamOnly.

briefdescription.para: Specifies 1944 x 2160 resolution. Aspect ratio: 9x10. MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_60FPS is not supported for this quality mode.
Specifies 2880 x 2160 resolution. Aspect ratio: 4x3. MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_60FPS is not supported for this quality mode.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Video Quality enumeration for mixed reality capture.

detaileddescription.para: Use MLCameraGetStreamCaps to get the resolution.
Camera only frame capture.
Virtual only capture. Only supported for MLCameraIdentifier_MAIN.
Mixed Reality capture. Only supported for MLCameraIdentifier_MAIN.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Flags to describe various modules in camera pipeline.
Additive blend type. It simply adds pixel values of real world and virtual layer.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Virtual and real content blending modes.
Device lost.
Priority lost.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Camera disconnect reason.
Invalid/Unknown Error.
Camera disabled.
Camera device failed.
Camera service failed.
Capture failed.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Camera errors.
YUV planar format.
Compressed output stream.
RGB32 format.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Captured output format. These three parameters determine which formats are supported:

detaileddescription.para:
These are formats supported:
Format
Camera Type
Mode
Capture Type
YUV
Main CV
Real Real
Preview, Video, Image Video, Image
RGBA
Main Main CR
Real MR, Virtual Real
Video Video, Image Video
JPEG
Main
Real, MR, Virtual
Image
Connected.
Idle.
Opened.
Disconnected.
Error. Call MLCameraGetErrorCode to obtain the error code.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Client can implement polling mechanism to retrieve device status and use these masks to view device status.
Number of planes representing the image color space.
Default distortion vector size.
Logical camera identifiers available for access.

Call MLCameraPrepareCapture() to configure frame rate. Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_None when configuring only Image capture. Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_60fps only when resolution of captures <= 3MP(2048x1536). Use MLCameraCaptureFrameRate_15FPS only when MLCameraConnectFlag is MLCameraConnectFlag_CamOnly.

briefdescription.para: Video Quality enumeration for mixed reality capture.

detaileddescription.para: Use MLCameraGetStreamCaps to get the resolution.
Flags to describe various modules in camera pipeline.
Virtual and real content blending modes.
Camera disconnect reason.
Camera errors.

briefdescription.para: Captured output format. These three parameters determine which formats are supported:

detaileddescription.para:
These are formats supported:
Format
Camera Type
Mode
Capture Type
YUV
Main CV
Real Real
Preview, Video, Image Video, Image
RGBA
Main Main CR
Real MR, Virtual Real
Video Video, Image Video
JPEG
Main
Real, MR, Virtual
Image
Client can implement polling mechanism to retrieve device status and use these masks to view device status.
Camera context to be used while connecting to camera.
A structure to encapsulate connection settings for MR capture.
A structure to encapsulate context for a CameraConnect Request.

briefdescription.para: A structure to encapsulate stream capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: The capabilities for each stream supported by logical camera device can be queried with MLCameraGetStreamCaps.
A structure to encapsulate stream configurations.
A structure to encapsulate capture configuration.
Per plane info for captured output.
A structure to encapsulate captured output.
A structure to encapsulate various indices for a capture result.
A structure to represent info on camera availability.
Device availability status callbacks to be implemented by client to receive device availability status.
Device status callbacks to be implemented by client to receive device status if callback mechanism is used.

briefdescription.para: Capture callbacks to be implemented by client to receive capture status if callback mechanism is used.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLCameraCaptureCallbacksInit() before use.
Shall be called before calling MLCameraConnect().
Shall be called before calling MLCameraPrepareCapture().
Shall be called before calling MLCameraSetDeviceAvailabilityStatusCallbacks().

parameterdescription.para: Device availability status callbacks structure to initialize.

detaileddescription.para: Shall be called before calling MLCameraSetDeviceStatusCallbacks().

parameterdescription.para: Device status callbacks structure to initialize.

detaileddescription.para: Shall be called before calling MLCameraSetCaptureCallbacks().

parameterdescription.para: Capture status callbacks structure to initialize.

briefdescription.para: Initialize ML Camera API, Register callback for device availability. If the user does not want to register device availability listener, MLCameraConnect can be called directly without MLCameraInit(). MLCameraDeInit should be used for unregistering callbacks and releasing resources acquired in MLCameraInit().

parameterdescription.para: Callback to notify camera availability status.
Pointer to user context data (can be NULL).
Camera is initialized and callbacks registered successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Camera context to be used in later APIs.
One of the parameters is invalid.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

briefdescription.para: Query the no of streams supported by camera device.

parameterdescription.para: Number of streams supported by device.
One of the parameters is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (MLCameraContext context, const uint32_t stream_index, uint32_t *inout_num_stream_caps, MLCameraCaptureStreamCaps *inout_stream_caps)
memberdef.name: MLCameraGetStreamCaps

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves either the number of capabilities supported by the given stream or an array of capabilities for the given stream. To get the number of capabilities supported by the given stream, this API should be called with num_stream_caps being valid pointer and out_stream_caps being NULL. To retrieve the array of capabilities for the given stream, this API should be called with a valid num_stream_caps (that contains the size of the out_stream_caps) and out_stream_caps being a preallocated array of MLCameraCaptureStreamCaps of size specified by num_stream_caps.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the stream for which to query capabilities. The index should be in the range [0 , num_supported_streams) where the num_supported_streams is from MLCameraGetNumSupportedStreams().

When the parameter inout_stream_caps is null, this is an output parameter which will hold the number of capabilities supported by the stream on function return. Otherwise this is an input parameter specifying the number of capabilities to retrieve, namely the size of the array pointed to by inout_stream_caps on return. The value should be in the range of [0, number-of-caps-retrieved].
This is either null or pointing to an array of MLCameraCaptureStreamCaps() on return. Note that caller is responsible for allocating and releasing the array.
One of the parameters is invalid.
Camera device already disconnected or camera device is streaming.

briefdescription.para: Uninitialize ML Camera API, unregister callback for device availability. Should be called after all camera devices are disconnected. After MLCameraDeInit, MLCameraInit can be called or MLCameraConnect can be called.

parameterdescription.para: DeInit called when camera device is connected.

detaileddescription.para: This API triggers AEAWB sequence and returns when AEAWB convergence has been achieved. The Users can either trigger and converge AEAWB metadata settings or use this API.

parameterdescription.para: Timed out waiting for AEAWB convergence.
Camera device not configured using MLCameraPrepareCapture() or camera device is streaming.

detaileddescription.para: This API prepares for capture per specified MLCameraCaptureType by creating a capture request, and a handle to which is returned to the user, who can choose to manipulate the request data (metadata) via APIs defined in ml_camera_metadata_v2.h before performing the capture.
Shall be called after MLCameraConnect().
Note: When preparing capture the width and height parameters should match one of the values returned from MLCameraGetStreamCaps.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the capture request metadata. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
Failed to prepare for capture due to invalid input parameter.
Failed to prepare for capture due to internal error.

detaileddescription.para: This API updates the capture settings for an active preview/video stream. The API can be used to update capture settings like exposure compensation manual exposure settings like exposure time, iso setting.The capture settings can be changed in metadata handle returned by MLCameraPrepareCapture() and then this API can be called to apply the capture settings.

parameterdescription.para: Camera not streaming video or not in preview.

briefdescription.para: Set the client-implemented callbacks to convey camera device status.

detaileddescription.para: Client needs to implement the callbacks defined by MLCameraDeviceStatusCallbacks. The library passes the camera device status to the client via those callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Set the client-implemented callbacks to convey capture status.

detaileddescription.para: Client needs to implement the callbacks defined by MLCameraCaptureCallbacks. The library passes the capture status to the client via those callbacks.
Shall be called before capture operation for preview/image/video.
The output image will be returned in capture callback on_image_buffer_available.
This is a blocking call, it will return after the end of on_image_buffer_available callback execution.

parameterdescription.para: Number of images to capture valid range is 1-10.
MLMediaGenericResult_InvalidOperation Capture device in invalid state or image stream not configured by MLCameraPrepareCapture().

briefdescription.para: Start video capture. Capture either encoded video or YUV/RGBA frames.

detaileddescription.para: YUV/RGBA frames are provides through callback.For encoded video capture valid encoder input surface should be configured.The captured video YUV/RGBA frames will be returned to the application via on_video_buffer_available.
MLCameraCaptureVideoStop needs to be called to stop the capture.

parameterdescription.para: Capture device in invalid state or video stream not configured by MLCameraPrepareCapture().
Failed to start video recording due to internal error.

briefdescription.para: Start preview provide raw frames through callback.

detaileddescription.para: The captured preview YUV frames will be returned to the application via on_preview_buffer_available.
MLCameraCapturePreviewStop needs to be called to stop the capture.

parameterdescription.para: Failed to start preview due to internal error.
Capture device in invalid state or preview stream not configured by MLCameraPrepareCapture().

detaileddescription.para: User should allow some time, i.e., >500ms, after MLCameraCaptureVideoStart and before calling this API, as captured frames are being encoded. Otherwise, MLResult_UnspecifiedFailure will be returned.

parameterdescription.para: Failed to stop video recording due to internal error.
Capture device in invalid state or video stream not streaming.
Failed to stop preview due to internal error.
Capture device in invalid state or preview stream not streaming.

detaileddescription.para: Use MLCameraDeviceStatusFlag to view specific status bit. Call MLCameraGetErrorCode() to obtain the error code if MLCameraDeviceStatusFlag_Error bit is set.
Note: This API can still be used even if MLCameraSetDeviceStatusCallbacks() has been called.

parameterdescription.para: Failed to obtain device status due to invalid input parameter.

detaileddescription.para: Use MLCameraGetDeviceAvailabilityStatus to view specific status.
Note: This API can still be used even if availability status callback is not registered through MLCameraInit call.

parameterdescription.para: Camera Id for which the availability status is to be queried.
Failed to obtain device status due to invalid input parameter.
Failed to obtain device error code due to invalid input parameter.
Obtained camera device error code successfully.

briefdescription.para: Obtains handle for retrieving camera characteristics.

detaileddescription.para: This API provides the handle for retrieving camera characteristics via APIs defined in ml_camera_metadata_v2.h.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to access camera characteristic metadata. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
Failed to obtain camera characteristic handle due to invalid input parameter.
